smith_skate.name: Smithsonian National Museum of American History

items: Reading: HTTP stands for Hypertext Transfer Protocol, the foundation for exchanging resources on the web.
Robotics: Student teams combine design, programming, fabrication, testing, and project management.
Rowing: Feathering rotates the oar blade nearly parallel to the water during recovery.
Salesmanship: Ethical selling includes truthful claims and respect for consumer-protection laws.
Scouting Heritage: Eleven of the twelve people who walked on the Moon had participated in Scouting.
Scuba Diving: A submersible pressure gauge shows how much breathing gas remains in a cylinder.
Skating: The ollie is a foundational skateboard jump used as the basis for many later tricks.
Small-Boat Sailing: Marlinespike seamanship includes practical knots, bends, hitches, and splices.
Snow Sports: Smart Style teaches terrain-park users to make a plan, look before leaping, and respect others.
Soil and Water Conservation: An aquifer is permeable rock or sediment that stores and transmits groundwater.
Space Exploration: NASA lost the Mars Climate Orbiter after one team used metric units and another used English units.
Sports: Warming up prepares the body for activity; cooling down helps heart rate and breathing return gradually.
Stamp Collecting: A first-day cover bears a stamp canceled on its official first day of issue.
Surveying: Modern GPS observations help establish precise positions and improve national mapping control.
Sustainability: Ecological overshoot occurs when demand on nature exceeds what ecosystems can regenerate.
Textile: Fibers and clothing have environmental impacts across production, use, reuse, and disposal.
Theater: A proscenium arch frames the stage opening and separates the audience from the playing space.
Traffic Safety: Bright and reflective clothing helps bicyclists become more visible to drivers.
Truck Transportation: Trucks connect farms, factories, warehouses, stores, ports, and homes across the freight network.
Veterinary Medicine: Veterinary technicians need specialized education and assist with tests, treatment, and animal care.
Water Sports: The HELP position reduces heat loss while a person waits for rescue in cold water.
Whitewater: A peel-out moves a boat from an eddy into the main current under control.
Wilderness Survival: A heavy-duty trash bag can become emergency rain protection or part of a shelter.
Woodwork: Species, moisture, grain, grading, and dimensions all affect how wood performs in a project.
Composite Materials: Combining fibers and a matrix can produce structures with high strength for their weight.
